About Me

Technique meets self-expression.

A versatile dancer, choreographer and instructor with 5–6 years of teaching experience. I blend technical precision with creativity and self-expression to help students of all ages build confidence, develop strong foundations and discover their own unique style through movement — across choreography, freestyle and performance, at every level.

Dance isn't just about learning steps; it's a way of life. Let's explore this journey together, dive into fresh movements, and unlock your unique flavour.
0Years Experience
0Students Taught
0Styles Coached

My Dance Styles

A wide vocabulary of movement.

From street foundations to stage choreography — hover a style to see how it fits into your training.

🔥

Hip Hop

The foundation — Bounce, Rock, groove and the culture that started it all.

⚡

Freestyle

Move without a script. Improvisation, instinct and personal flavour.

🎬

Open Style Choreo

Genre-blending routines built around a song and a story.

🦶

Light Feet

Fast, rhythmic footwork that sharpens control and timing.

🔒

Locking

Crisp freezes and funk energy — playful, musical and precise.

🤖

Popping

Hits, waves and illusions built on muscle control and sharp timing.

📐

Tutting

Geometric shapes and angles — the body as living architecture.

🌊

Waving

Fluid illusions that make movement ripple through the body.

💃

Bollywood

Expressive, high-energy fusion — perfect for weddings and shows.

🔗

Art of Connection

Dancing with the music, the moment and the people watching.

🎵

Musicality

Hearing and hitting the music — the soul of real dancing.

✨

Creative Movement

Exploration and play that unlock your own way of moving.

🎤

Performance Training

Stage presence, projection and the confidence to own a room.

The Art of Connection

Dance is connection made visible.

Technique is only the start. Real dancing is about understanding music, emotion, body control and expression — and connecting them all at once. That's what I teach beyond the steps.

To the music

Feeling rhythm, texture and silence — so you move with a song, not just on top of it.

To movement

Body control, weight and flow that make every motion intentional.

To yourself

Finding your own voice and flavour instead of copying anyone else.

To the audience

Presence and expression that make people feel what you feel.

To students

Meeting each dancer where they are and growing with them, session by session.

Teaching Philosophy

How I teach — and why it works.

5 Years · Academy Instructor

Teaching every kind of dancer

Full-time studio experience across a wide range of ages and levels — I adapt the same foundations to whoever is in front of me.

ChildrenTeenagersAdultsBeginnersIntermediateAdvanced

The Approach

Culture first, confidence always

Every style comes with the history and culture behind it, taught in a positive, supportive space where students of all ages grow with confidence.

The Outcome

Your own style, on solid technique

Strong fundamentals and body control that never limit creativity — so you leave able to move, improvise and perform as yourself.

  • Teach the history and culture behind every style
  • Build confidence through movement and performance
  • Develop creativity and artistic expression
  • Encourage individuality — help each student find their style
  • Improve musicality: rhythm, timing and groove
  • Strengthen freestyle through exploration and improvisation
  • Build technique without limiting self-expression
  • Enhance body control, balance and coordination
  • Improve dance fitness — strength, endurance, mobility
  • Keep a positive, supportive space for every age

Choreography

Routines built from scratch.

Original work for stages, competitions, brands and celebrations — shaped around your music, your people and your moment.

  • Original Hip Hop choreography
  • Competition choreography
  • Corporate shows
  • Theme performances
  • Student performances
  • Custom dance projects

Every piece follows the same creative process — so the final performance feels intentional from the first count to the last.

01

Idea

The concept, feeling and goal behind the piece.

02

Music

Choosing and editing the track that drives it.

03

Concept

Structure, formations and the story arc.

04

Practice

Cleaning, drilling and building confidence.

05

Performance

Owning the stage when it counts.
